ChatGPT said:

Perfect — this error simply means:

Node.js is NOT installed yet

(or it wasn’t added to PATH correctly).

Because:

npm is not recognized

means Windows cannot find npm.

And npm automatically comes WITH Node.js.

So this is very easy to fix.

STEP 1 — INSTALL NODE.JS

Go here:

Node.js Official Download

STEP 2 — DOWNLOAD THIS VERSION

Choose:

→ LTS Version

NOT Current.

The LTS one is more stable.

STEP 3 — IMPORTANT DURING INSTALLATION

When installing:

✅ Keep clicking “Next”

BUT VERY IMPORTANT:

Make sure this is checked:

Add to PATH

Usually it is already checked.

DO NOT uncheck it.

STEP 4 — RESTART VS CODE

After installation:

  • close VS Code completely

  • reopen VS Code

  • reopen your project folder

This part is IMPORTANT because VS Code needs to refresh PATH variables.

STEP 5 — CHECK IF NODE IS INSTALLED

Open terminal again and type:

Bash
node -v

You should see something like:

Bash
v22.x.x

Then type:

Bash
npm -v

You should see:

Bash
10.x.x

NOW it’s working.

STEP 6 — NOW RUN THIS

Since your folder already exists:

Bash
npm create vite@latest .

IMPORTANT:
Use the dot (.)

THEN SELECT

Framework → React
Variant → JavaScript

AFTER THAT

Run:

Bash
npm install
npm run dev

And your project officially begins 🚀


You said:

I have installed Node. So now after I have installed Node, what do I do again? Let's go back to VS Code. Now what should I do?

ChatGPT said:

Perfect. Now we continue from VS Code. 🚀

STEP 1 — REOPEN VS CODE

Close and reopen VS Code completely after Node installation.

Then:

Open your project folder again:

File → Open Folder → NOVA chatbot

STEP 2 — OPEN TERMINAL

In VS Code:

Terminal → New Terminal

You should now see something like:

Bash
PS C:\Users\ritha\OneDrive\Desktop\NOVA chatbot>

GOOD.

STEP 3 — CHECK NODE IS WORKING

Type this:

Bash
node -v

Press Enter.

You should see something like:

Bash
v22.x.x

Then type:

Bash
npm -v

You should see a version number.

If BOTH work:
✅ everything is installed correctly.

STEP 4 — CREATE THE REACT PROJECT

NOW type:

Bash
npm create vite@latest .

IMPORTANT:
Use the DOT (.)

because your folder already exists.

STEP 5 — ANSWER THE QUESTIONS

It will ask:

Framework:

Choose:

React

Use arrow keys + Enter.

Variant:

Choose:

JavaScript

STEP 6 — INSTALL DEPENDENCIES

After setup finishes, type:

Bash
npm install

Wait until it completes.

STEP 7 — RUN THE PROJECT

Now type:

Bash
npm run dev

You’ll see something like:

Local: http://localhost:5173/

STEP 8 — OPEN THE WEBSITE

Hold:

Ctrl

and click the localhost link.

OR copy-paste it into browser.

WHAT YOU SHOULD SEE

A Vite + React starter page.

That means:

YOUR PROJECT IS OFFICIALLY CREATED 🎉

AFTER THIS

Tell me:

  • whether it worked

  • or send screenshot/errors

Then we’ll immediately move to:

Tailwind setup + your actual futuristic UI.


You said:

Please download and install a JDK to compile your project. You can configure your projects with different JDKs by the setting 'java.configuration.runtimes'

ChatGPT said:

That message is completely fine — you can IGNORE it for now. ✅

It’s coming from the Java extension in VS Code, NOT from React/Vite.

You are building:

React + JavaScript

NOT Java.

So you do NOT need a JDK right now.

WHAT TO DO NOW

Go back to the terminal and continue normally.

Run:

Bash
node -v

Then:

Bash
npm -v

If both show version numbers, continue.

THEN RUN THIS

Bash
npm create vite@latest .

Remember the dot (.).

THEN SELECT

React

and then:

JavaScript

AFTER THAT

Run:

Bash
npm install

Then:

Bash
npm run dev

That Java/JDK message is unrelated to your project. Ignore it for now.


You said:

Need to install the following packages: create-vite@9.0.7 Ok to proceed? (y)

ChatGPT said:

Yes — type:

Bash
y

and press Enter. ✅

That’s completely normal.

It’s just asking permission to install Vite temporarily to create your React project.

After that:

  • choose React

  • then choose JavaScript

Then continue with:

Bash
npm install

and then:

Bash
npm run dev

You’re almost at the point where your first app launches 🚀


You said:

> npx > create-vite . │ ◆ Package name: │ rithanya-chatbot

ChatGPT said:

Perfect — that means Vite is now creating your project. 🚀

Right now it’s asking:

Package name:

rithanya-chatbot

You can:

  • keep it as is
    OR

  • type a different name

I’d recommend:

nova-ai

OR

nova-student-ai

because it matches your product branding better.

IMPORTANT

After typing the name:
Press:

Enter

Then it will ask:

Framework

Choose:

React

(using arrow keys + Enter)

Then:

Variant

Choose:

JavaScript

After that finishes:

Run:

Bash
npm install

Wait for installation to complete.

Then finally:

Bash
npm run dev

You’re VERY close now.


ChatGPT said:

Choose:

JavaScript

NOT:

  • TypeScript

  • React Compiler

  • RSC

  • Router options

Right now you want the simplest and cleanest setup possible.

So use arrow keys ↓ and select:

→ JavaScript

Then press Enter. ✅

PS C:\Users\ritha\OneDrive\Desktop\Rithanya Chatbot> npm create vite@latest . Need to install the following packages: create-vite@9.0.7 Ok to proceed? (y) y > npx > create-vite . │ ◇ Package name: │ nova-ai │ ◇ Select a framework: │ React │ ◇ Select a variant: │ JavaScript │ ◇ Install with npm and start now? │ Yes │ ◇ Scaffolding project in C:\Users\ritha\OneDrive\Desktop\Rithanya Chatbot... │ ◇ Installing dependencies with npm... added 135 packages, and audited 136 packages in 24s 31 packages are looking for funding run npm fund for details found 0 vulnerabilities │ ◇ Starting dev server... > nova-ai@0.0.0 dev > vite VITE v8.0.14 ready in 533 ms ➜ Local: http://localhost:5173/ ➜ Network: use --host to expose ➜ press h + enter to show help

ChatGPT said:

PERFECT. 🎉
That means your React app is working correctly.

The:

Get started
Edit src/App.jsx
Count is 0

screen is just the default Vite starter page.

Now we replace it with YOUR actual app.

STEP 1 — OPEN src/App.jsx

Delete EVERYTHING inside it.

Replace it with this:

JavaScript
function App() {
  return (
    <div>
      <h1>Welcome to NOVA AI</h1>
    </div>
  )
}

export default App

STEP 2 — SAVE THE FILE

Press:

Ctrl + S

The browser updates automatically.

Now you should ONLY see:

Welcome to NOVA AI

on the page.

STEP 3 — CLEAN THE STYLES

Now open:

src/index.css

Delete EVERYTHING inside it.

Replace with:

CSS
body {
  margin: 0;
  padding: 0;
  font-family: sans-serif;
}

Save.

STEP 4 — CREATE FOLDERS

Inside src/

Create:

components
screens
assets
styles

STEP 5 — CREATE THE WELCOME SCREEN FILE

Inside:

src/screens

Create:

WelcomeScreen.jsx

STEP 6 — PASTE THIS INTO WelcomeScreen.jsx

JavaScript
function WelcomeScreen() {
  return (
    <div>
      <h1>Welcome to NOVA AI</h1>
    </div>
  )
}

export default WelcomeScreen

STEP 7 — CONNECT IT TO APP.JSX

Now replace App.jsx with:

JavaScript
import WelcomeScreen from "./screens/WelcomeScreen"

function App() {
  return <WelcomeScreen />
}

export default App

Save.

NOW YOUR PROJECT STRUCTURE IS CLEAN

And we’re officially ready to start designing the REAL futuristic UI next. 🚀

your app is working perfectly.

installing Tailwind CSS

because WITHOUT Tailwind,
your UI will be very hard to style beautifully.

STEP 1 — KEEP YOUR DEV SERVER RUNNING

DO NOT close this terminal:

Bash
npm run dev

Leave it running.

STEP 2 — OPEN A NEW TERMINAL

In VS Code top menu:

Terminal → New Terminal

Now you’ll have TWO terminals:

  • one running app

  • one for commands

STEP 3 — INSTALL TAILWIND

In the NEW terminal type:

Bash
npm install tailwindcss @tailwindcss/vite

Press Enter.

Wait until it finishes.

STEP 4 — THEN INSTALL THESE

After that completes, run:

Bash
npm install framer-motion lucide-react

WHY WE’RE DOING THIS

Tailwind CSS

Makes beautiful UI MUCH easier.

Framer Motion

For smooth animations.

Lucide React

For modern icons.

AFTER INSTALLATION

Tell me:

“done”

Then I’ll guide you through:

connecting Tailwind properly

AND THEN:
we’ll finally begin building your real futuristic UI.

You said:

yes it came npw what to do

ChatGPT said:

Perfectttt 🚀
Tailwind is now installed.

Now we CONNECT Tailwind to your React app.

Follow VERY carefully.

STEP 1 — OPEN vite.config.js

In your project files, find:

vite.config.js

Click it.

STEP 2 — REPLACE EVERYTHING INSIDE IT

Delete everything.

Paste this:

JavaScript
import { defineConfig } from 'vite'
import react from '@vitejs/plugin-react'
import tailwindcss from '@tailwindcss/vite'

export default defineConfig({
  plugins: [
    react(),
    tailwindcss(),
  ],
})

Save it.
(Ctrl + S)

STEP 3 — OPEN src/index.css

Delete EVERYTHING inside it.

Paste this:

CSS
@import "tailwindcss";

Save.

STEP 4 — TEST IF TAILWIND WORKS

Now open:

src/App.jsx

Replace everything with:

JavaScript
function App() {
  return (
    <div className="h-screen bg-purple-200 flex items-center justify-center">
      <h1 className="text-5xl font-bold text-purple-800">
        Welcome to NOVA AI
      </h1>
    </div>
  )
}

export default App

Save.

WHAT SHOULD HAPPEN

Your page should NOW become:

✅ full purple background
✅ centered text
✅ large bold title

instead of plain HTML styling.

That means:

Tailwind is working successfully 🎉

AFTER YOU SEE THAT

Tell me:

“done”

Then we’ll start building:

the REAL futuristic pastel AI welcome screen ✨


You said:

cThis is the VIP Cognify JSON. So after that, you want me to open the index.css file. So in this index.css file, it's given like a really long code, no? I mean, like, they've given like colors of like, they've mentioned like fonts, et cetera. So you want me to delete absolutely everything and only put that import payment CSS? Are you sure? Is that what you want me to do?

ChatGPT said:

YES — for THIS new Tailwind version, that is correct. ✅

The long code you’re seeing inside index.css is just Vite’s default starter styling.

Things like:

  • fonts

  • button styles

  • colors

  • root settings

  • light/dark mode defaults

Those belong to the starter template.

We do NOT want those because:

we are building our OWN UI from scratch.

So yes:

delete everything inside src/index.css

and replace it ONLY with:

CSS
@import "tailwindcss";

That’s correct for Tailwind v4 (the version you installed).

WHY WE DELETE THE DEFAULT CODE

Because otherwise:

  • default button styles interfere

  • unwanted fonts appear

  • strange margins happen

  • Vite’s demo theme affects your UI

We want:

a completely clean canvas.

AFTER YOU SAVE

Go back to:

App.jsx

and paste the Tailwind test code I gave earlier:

JavaScript
function App() {
  return (
    <div className="h-screen bg-purple-200 flex items-center justify-center">
      <h1 className="text-5xl font-bold text-purple-800">
        Welcome to NOVA AI
      </h1>
    </div>
  )
}

export default App

Save it.

IF EVERYTHING WORKS

Your webpage becomes:

  • full light purple background

  • centered large text

  • styled automatically

Then Tailwind is working correctly.

You said:

The page didn't turn into anything, the page is just now completely blank. Now, so now what?

ChatGPT said:

That’s okay — it usually means there’s a SMALL syntax issue somewhere.
Very normal while setting up React + Tailwind for the first time.

We’ll fix it step by step.

FIRST — CHECK THE TERMINAL

Look at the terminal where:

Bash
npm run dev

is running.

Do you see:

  • red error messages?

  • warnings?

  • anything saying “failed”?

If yes, send me the screenshot or copy the error.

SECOND — CHECK App.jsx

Your ENTIRE App.jsx should be EXACTLY this:

JavaScript
function App() {
  return (
    <div className="h-screen bg-purple-200 flex items-center justify-center">
      <h1 className="text-5xl font-bold text-purple-800">
        Welcome to NOVA AI
      </h1>
    </div>
  )
}

export default App

Make sure:
✅ brackets match
✅ spelling matches exactly
✅ no extra symbols
✅ no deleted export line

THIRD — CHECK index.css

Inside:

src/index.css

there should ONLY be:

CSS
@import "tailwindcss";

Nothing else.

FOURTH — CHECK main.jsx

Open:

src/main.jsx

Make sure THIS line exists:

JavaScript
import './index.css'

VERY IMPORTANT.

If that line is missing,
Tailwind styles won’t load.

MOST LIKELY ISSUE

Usually blank page means:

  • missing bracket

  • syntax mistake

  • export missing

  • accidental deletion

NOT a major problem.

EASIEST WAY NOW

Send me:

  1. your App.jsx

  2. your main.jsx

and I’ll immediately tell you the exact issue.

ChatGPT said:

GOOD. This error is actually very helpful — now we know EXACTLY what went wrong.

The issue is:

Tailwind did NOT install properly.

That’s why it says:

Cannot find package '@tailwindcss/vite'

So we fix it very easily.

STEP 1 — STOP THE SERVER

In the terminal where npm run dev is running:

Press:

Ctrl + C

Then type:

y

if it asks.

This stops the dev server.

STEP 2 — INSTALL TAILWIND PROPERLY

In the terminal run EXACTLY this:

Bash
npm install tailwindcss @tailwindcss/vite postcss autoprefixer

WAIT until it fully finishes.

STEP 3 — CHECK vite.config.js

Open:

vite.config.js

and make sure it is EXACTLY this:

JavaScript
import { defineConfig } from 'vite'
import react from '@vitejs/plugin-react'
import tailwindcss from '@tailwindcss/vite'

export default defineConfig({
  plugins: [
    react(),
    tailwindcss(),
  ],
})

Save.

STEP 4 — CHECK src/index.css

Delete everything.

Paste ONLY:

CSS
@import "tailwindcss";

Save.

STEP 5 — START SERVER AGAIN

Run:

Bash
npm run dev

again.

STEP 6 — CHECK PAGE

If everything works,
your purple Tailwind screen should appear.

WHY THIS HAPPENED

Completely normal.

Usually:

  • package didn’t finish installing

  • or terminal restarted midway

  • or Tailwind dependency missing

NOT a serious issue at all.

ChatGPT said:

You’re VERY close. Don’t worry — the main problem is simply that Tailwind never finished installing properly.

And the reason y failed at the end is because you typed it separately after the command already ended 😭
That’s not an issue.

Now let’s fix this CLEANLY.

STEP 1 — STOP EVERYTHING

Close:

  • all terminals

  • the browser tab

  • VS Code completely

YES completely.

STEP 2 — REOPEN VS CODE

Open:

Rithanya Chatbot

project folder again.

STEP 3 — OPEN A NEW TERMINAL

Inside VS Code:

Terminal → New Terminal

STEP 4 — INSTALL TAILWIND PROPERLY

NOW copy-paste THIS EXACT command:

Bash
npm install tailwindcss @tailwindcss/vite postcss autoprefixer

IMPORTANT:
Wait patiently until installation COMPLETELY finishes.

You should see something like:

added packages...
found 0 vulnerabilities

NO red errors.

STEP 5 — FIX vite.config.js

Open:

vite.config.js

Delete everything.

Paste EXACTLY this:

JavaScript
import { defineConfig } from 'vite'
import react from '@vitejs/plugin-react'
import tailwindcss from '@tailwindcss/vite'

export default defineConfig({
  plugins: [
    react(),
    tailwindcss(),
  ],
})

Save.

STEP 6 — FIX src/index.css

Delete everything inside it.

Paste ONLY:

CSS
@import "tailwindcss";

Save.

STEP 7 — FIX src/App.jsx

Paste EXACTLY this:

JavaScript
function App() {
  return (
    <div className="h-screen bg-purple-200 flex items-center justify-center">
      <h1 className="text-5xl font-bold text-purple-800">
        Welcome to NOVA AI
      </h1>
    </div>
  )
}

export default App

Save.

STEP 8 — START SERVER AGAIN

In terminal run:

Bash
npm run dev

STEP 9 — OPEN WEBSITE

Open:

http://localhost:5173

WHAT SHOULD HAPPEN

You should FINALLY see:

✅ light purple full screen
✅ centered “Welcome to NOVA AI”
✅ large bold purple text

Then Tailwind is officially working.


You said:

This is the previous Chatbot, which was inside a chatbot, but I changed the things, so I deleted that company folder and created a new folder called NovaBot. So after that, I have gone to the terminal. I've created a new terminal. So basically, there's pretty much nothing there left, so you only want me to do the npm install tailwind CSS, or then, or should I also install something else? Like I deleted everything, so I'm starting from the beginning scratch. So are there anything else that I should install or add? Generally, just tell me what everything needs to be installed. So first, let's install everything, and then let's start, you know, implementing everything. Got it?

ChatGPT said:

YES — this is actually MUCH better. ✅
Starting fresh in a clean folder is the correct decision.

Now we’ll do the ENTIRE setup properly from the beginning so nothing breaks later.

STEP 1 — CREATE THE PROJECT

Inside your NEW folder (NovaBot):

Open terminal and run:

Bash
npm create vite@latest .

IMPORTANT:
Use the dot (.)

SELECT:

Framework

React

Variant

JavaScript

STEP 2 — INSTALL MAIN PROJECT DEPENDENCIES

After project creation finishes,
run ALL of these one by one.

INSTALL TAILWIND + POSTCSS

Bash
npm install tailwindcss @tailwindcss/vite postcss autoprefixer

INSTALL ANIMATION LIBRARY

Bash
npm install framer-motion

INSTALL ICONS

Bash
npm install lucide-react

OPTIONAL BUT HIGHLY RECOMMENDED

For beautiful utilities later:

Bash
npm install clsx tailwind-merge

STEP 3 — CONFIGURE TAILWIND

Now open:

vite.config.js

Replace EVERYTHING with:

JavaScript
import { defineConfig } from 'vite'
import react from '@vitejs/plugin-react'
import tailwindcss from '@tailwindcss/vite'

export default defineConfig({
  plugins: [
    react(),
    tailwindcss(),
  ],
})

Save.

STEP 4 — CONFIGURE CSS

Open:

src/index.css

Delete everything.

Paste ONLY:

CSS
@import "tailwindcss";

Save.

STEP 5 — CLEAN APP.JSX

Open:

src/App.jsx

Delete everything.

Paste:

JavaScript
function App() {
  return (
    <div className="h-screen bg-purple-200 flex items-center justify-center">
      <h1 className="text-5xl font-bold text-purple-800">
        Welcome to NOVA AI
      </h1>
    </div>
  )
}

export default App

Save.

STEP 6 — START THE SERVER

Run:

Bash
npm run dev

STEP 7 — OPEN WEBSITE

Open:

http://localhost:5173

IF EVERYTHING WORKS

You should see:

✅ full light purple screen
✅ centered large purple text
✅ “Welcome to NOVA AI”

THEN:
your entire environment is PERFECTLY set up.

AFTER THIS

We begin the REAL exciting part:

✨ Building your futuristic pastel AI interface

including:

  • soft gradients

  • glowing effects

  • floating cards

  • glassmorphism

  • animated AI orb

  • premium typography

  • smooth animations

  • dashboard UI

THAT’S when your project starts looking insane.


You said:

I have my local link. Okay, so after creating my local link, I'm able to press on that local link. And after I have created that local link, what do you want me to do next? Okay, so this has been created. Now, where should I put the install tailwind post CSS? Where should I type that? Where should I type the animation? Where should I install all of this? Like, what do I do?

ChatGPT said:

Perfect. You are at the CORRECT stage now. 🚀

Right now:

npm run dev

is running successfully and your local link works.

GOOD.

Now listen carefully:

VERY IMPORTANT

You will ALWAYS have:

TWO TERMINALS

TERMINAL 1

This terminal runs your website.

It contains:

Bash
npm run dev

DO NOT close this terminal.

Leave it running.

NOW CREATE:

TERMINAL 2

Inside VS Code top menu:

Terminal → New Terminal

A NEW terminal opens below.

THIS is where you install packages.

NOW TYPE INSTALL COMMANDS IN TERMINAL 2

First paste:

Bash
npm install tailwindcss @tailwindcss/vite postcss autoprefixer

Press Enter.

Wait until installation fully completes.

THEN TYPE:

Bash
npm install framer-motion

Press Enter.

THEN TYPE:

Bash
npm install lucide-react

Press Enter.

THAT’S IT

You are basically:

  • keeping website running in Terminal 1

  • installing packages in Terminal 2

This is the normal professional workflow.

AFTER ALL INSTALLATIONS FINISH

THEN:
we modify:

  • vite.config.js

  • index.css

  • App.jsx

to activate Tailwind and start the real UI.

So right now:

ONLY install the packages first.

ChatGPT said:

NOOO 😭
You do NOT need more terminals now.

You already have the correct setup.

RIGHT NOW YOU SHOULD HAVE:

TERMINAL 1

Running:

Bash
npm run dev

Leave this running.

TERMINAL 2

Where you just installed Tailwind.

Use THIS SAME terminal for the remaining installs.

No need to create another one.

NOW IN TERMINAL 2 RUN:

Bash
npm install framer-motion

Wait for it to finish.

THEN RUN:

Bash
npm install lucide-react

Wait for it to finish.

AFTER THAT

Tell me:

“done”

Then we’ll:

  1. activate Tailwind

  2. configure the files

JavaScript
import { defineConfig } from 'vite'
import react from '@vitejs/plugin-react'
import tailwindcss from '@tailwindcss/vite'

  plugins: [
    react(),
    tailwindcss(),
  ],
})
